English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Daniel and Philip Berrigan Collection
Berrigan, Daniel.
Berrigan, Philip.
4602
207 cubic feet. 207 cubic feet.
1880-1995. Correspondence, legal documents, manuscripts of poetry and prose writings, notes for talks, periodical articles, newspaper clippings, photographs, broadsides, tape recordings, videocassettes, microfilms. Personal papers and ephemeral publications by and about Daniel and Philip Berrigan. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD b86 b91 b93 b94 b95 b96 b97 ? (unprocessed) ======= b86 b91 b93 b94 b95 b96 b97 ? (unprocessed) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2024449
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
D. Randolph P. McCray staff
Date completed:
Additions Jan. 1996 Sep. 1997
EAD encoding:
Mireille Lee, August 2000
Date modified:
Jude Corina, December 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
The brothers Daniel Berrigan (born 1921) and Philip Berrigan (born 1923) have both been prominent in resistance to racial segregation, to U.S. involvement in the Vietnam war, and to the production of nuclear weapons. Philip Berrigan, a former Josephite priest, and Daniel, a Jesuit priest, poet, playwright, and teacher, are both lecturers and civil disobedience activists.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Personal papers and ephemeral publications by and about Daniel and Philip Berrigan, including correspondence, legal documents, manuscripts of poetry and prose writings, notes for talks, periodical arcticles, newspaper clippings, photographs, broadsides, tape recordings, videocassettes, and microfilms. The greater part of the collection consists of papers dated after 1960 and contains much on the Catonsville Nine, the Harrisburg Seven, and the Plowshares Eight. Earlier material includes some Berrigan family history. The collection is supplemented by published books by and about the Berrigans. Also twelve letters of correspondence with Ross Labrie.
Also a broadside from the "America is Hard to Find" weekend, April 17-19, 1970; and a videocassette "America is Hard to Find," - Spring 1970. Video: "The Trial of the Catonsville Nine," screenplay by Daniel Berrigan and Saul Levitt ; produced by Gregory Peck ; directed by Gordon Davidson, ca. 1988; and poster from the Actors' Gang/Office of the Americas (OOA) staged reading of "The Trial of the Catonsville Nine," August 18, 2007.
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
The collection comprises two series. Series A is documented to item level; please refer to the card catalogue or contact a Reference librarian. Series B comprises later accessions. Within Series B, only boxes B98 and B99 are described to folder or item level.
SERIES LIST
Bound Manuscripts
Bd. Ms. 1-12
OTHER GUIDES TO THE COLLECTION
A card catalogue is available providing item-level description of Series A of the collection.
Cite As:
Daniel and Philip Berrigan Collection, #4602. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Restrictions on Use:
Daniel Berrigan retains literary rights. Philip Berrigan does not retain literary rights.
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Audio visual material that has been digitized: V-209
Videotapes. Photographs. Student movements. Harrisburg Seven (Trial). Plowshares Eight (Group). Catonsville Nine -- Trials, litigation, etc. Vietnam War, 1961-1975 -- Protest movements. Social justice. Religion and politics -- United States. Radicalism -- United States. Radical theory. Politics in literature. Political poetry, American. Political plays. Political culture. Political activists. Protest poetry, American. Peace movements. Modernism -- Catholic Church. Civil rights movements. Antinuclear movement. Berrigan, Daniel. Berrigan, Philip. Berrigan, Jerome C. McAlister, Elizabeth. Labrie, Ross. Catholic Church -- : Political participation.

Daniel Berrigan Materials - 1979-1994, 19961979B1Jan. 1980 - Aug. 1980B2Jan. 1981 - Jun. 1981B31981Carbray GiftB4Jul. 1981 - Mar 1982B5Sept. 1982 - Dec 1983B61982, 1984B71983 - 1985B8Apr 1982 - Apr 1984B91984Marion Howe GiftB101984 - 1985B111985 - 1986B121986B131986B141987B15Sept. 1987 - Aug. 1988B161988B171988B181988 - 1989B191988 - 1989B201989 - 1990B211990 - 1991B221992B60TapesB61Tapes, Records, ManuscriptsB62
Scope and Contents
Includes the LPs titled Berrigan raps with notes by George Riemer, Side A 1.A sermon from the underground, 2. An interview with Marc N. Weiss at Cornell, April 1, 1970, Side B Father Berrigan reads his poetry 1. False gods, real men 2.Trial poems I 3. Trial poems II. The trial of the Catonsville nine directed by Gordon Davidson. Play performed by the Center Theatre Group for the Mark Taper Forum, Music Center, Los Angeles, text prepared for the production by Saul Levitt. America is hard to find, Side A is a reading of several poems by Daniel Berrigan. Side B is a rock mass perhaps without any involvement by Berrigan. Recorded February 27-March 2, 1970 in Ithaca, New York. Producers Steve Grunder and Steve Rathe, performers include Allan Sorvall (tracks: B4-B7), David Turner (12) (tracks: B4-B7), and John Hostetter.Tapes, John Deedy Gift, C-9 FilmB631991 - 1992B761992 - 1993B80Correspondence, Poetry, DiariesB92Photograph album including photographs of festivities and attendees of Daniel Berrigan 75th Birthday Celebration, 1996B99B. Philip Berrigan Materials - 1979-19941986B101979 - 1980B231979 - 1980B241980 - 1981B251980 - 1981B261981B271981B281981Ploughshares MaterialB291981B301981 - 1982B311982B321982B331982 - 1983B341983B351983B361983B371983B381984B391984B401984B411984B421984 - 1985B431984 - 1985B441985B451985B461985 - 1986B471986B481986B491986B501988B511988B521988 - 1989B531989B541989 - 1990B551990B561990B571991B581991B591991B60Engraved PhotographsB631991 - 1992Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB771991 - 1992Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB781991 - 1992Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB791992 - 1994Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB811992 - 1994Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB821992 - 1994Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB831992 - 1994Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB841992 - 1994Philip Berrigan, Elizabeth McalisterB851994B891994B90C. Miscellaneous1970s - 1980s - Miscellaneous BacklogB66Rosemary Bannan GiftB74Kairos GiftB75Mcvey FilesB88Frederic M. O'Connor, S.J. Material Correspondence, clippings, photocopies, and other material, c. 1964-70B9834-35"Identity Crisis and Monastic Vocation" by Thomas Merton, annotated by Daniel Berrigan; other Merton material, 1964B9836Isaiah typescript; poems, n.d.B9837G. Oversize MaterialOversize Material2America is Hard to Find Poster1H. VideoInvestigation of a FlameDVD-120B61The Trial of the Catonsville NineV-209B61
The Trial of the Catonsville Nine [Preservation]
The Trial of the Catonsville Nine [Staff access]America is Hard to FindV-8151970B61I. AudioAmerica is Hard to Find (LP)SR-340681 phonographic records.1970Series III. Bound ManuscriptsPurgatorioBd. Ms. 1
Scope and Contents
Mass market paper back, Oxford University Press edition of the work of Dante Alighieri, title page and some other pages removed, personal copy of Philip Berrigan, with pencilled annotations. Book has been removed from binding and individual sections bound with tape.Demonstration photo albumBd. Ms. 2 +
Scope and Contents
Album of photographs of Philip Berrigan and other people at several sites; no dates given; photographer/s unknown; various signs and banners displayed; some photos depict interactions with police. There is also a work of graphic art by George Knowlton.Prison and the Christian Resister: The Letters and Journals from Prison of Daniel and Philip BerriganBd. Ms. 3 +
Scope and Contents
M.A. Thesis SUNY Buffalo, pertains to the lives and activities, prior to 1976, of Daniel and Philip Berrigan, Catholic priests active in peace and social justice causes by Sara J. McLaughlin.Religion and radicalism: the American "Catholic Left" as a social movementBd. Ms. 4 +
Scope and Contents
PhD dissertation prepared at Columbia University by Charles A. Meconis. Found (with papers from Daniel Berrigan of early 1981) in a box labelled (by Meconis?) "to Prof. Gilllian Lindt from: Charles A. Meconis PhD. (Cand.) re: Defense copy of dissertation." With the dissertation were found typed and handwritten comments by Elizabeth (McAlister) Berrigan, Philip Francis Berrigan, and Daniel Berrigan; these have been removed and separately cataloged under Berrigan, Elizabeth (McAlister). The title page was lacking, and Philip Berrigan's comments were written on the copyright page; therefore, the dissertation has been bound with replacement title page and copyright page. Cf. the book (N.Y.: Seabury 1979) by Charles A. Meconis, With clumsy grace: the American Catholic left, 1961-1975.[Unused numbers]Bd. Ms. 5 & 6The PetreanBd. Ms. 7 + v.1-3
Scope and Contents
Yearbook of St. Peter's Preparatory School, Jersey City, New Jersey, 1947-1949.Blue Book, 1957Bd. Ms. 8 +
Scope and Contents
Yearbook of Brooklyn Preparatory School, Brooklyn, N.Y.Pity the nation..Bd. Ms. 9 +
Scope and Contents
This is a bound script of a drama about a 1969 trial in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. The author has signed the manuscript with a note to Daniel Berrigan, by James Sage.Daniel Berrigan, S.J.: A bibliography of criticism, with an introductory essay on his critical reputationBd. Ms. 10 +
Scope and Contents
MA thesis prepared at the University of Maryland by Carol Ann Rittner. Thesis is concerned with interpretation and critical reception of Daniel Berrigan's published works.Daniel Berrigan scrapbookBd. Ms. 11 ++
Scope and Contents
Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings, photos, programs, and writings assembled by Daniel Berrigan in his youth. There is material about various Berrigan family members: John Berrigan, Jerome Berrigan, Edmund Berrigan, Agnes Berrigan, etc. There are early writings by Daniel Berrigan and a poem by John Berrigan. Selection of clippings prefigures some of Daniel Berrigan's adult interests in civil rights and resistance to civil authority when in conflict with religious idealism.Daniel Berrigan poetryBd. Ms. 12 +
Scope and Contents
Consists of a collection of Daniel Berrigan's poetic works, copied and bound together. ======= Series IA. Daniel Berrigan Materials - 1979-1994, 19961979B1Jan. 1980 - Aug. 1980B2Jan. 1981 - Jun. 1981B31981Carbray GiftB4Jul. 1981 - Mar 1982B5Sept. 1982 - Dec 1983B61982, 1984B71983 - 1985B8Apr 1982 - Apr 1984B91984Marion Howe GiftB101984 - 1985B111985 - 1986B121986B131986B141987B15Sept. 1987 - Aug. 1988B161988B171988B181988 - 1989B191988 - 1989B201989 - 1990B211990 - 1991B221992B60TapesB61Tapes, Records, ManuscriptsB62
Scope and Contents
Includes the LPs titled Berrigan raps with notes by George Riemer, Side A 1.A sermon from the underground, 2. An interview with Marc N. Weiss at Cornell, April 1, 1970, Side B Father Berrigan reads his poetry 1. False gods, real men 2.Trial poems I 3. Trial poems II. The trial of the Catonsville nine directed by Gordon Davidson. Play performed by the Center Theatre Group for the Mark Taper Forum, Music Center, Los Angeles, text prepared for the production by Saul Levitt. America is hard to find, Side A is a reading of several poems by Daniel Berrigan. Side B is a rock mass perhaps without any involvement by Berrigan. Recorded February 27-March 2, 1970 in Ithaca, New York. Producers Steve Grunder and Steve Rathe, performers include Allan Sorvall (tracks: B4-B7), David Turner (12) (tracks: B4-B7), and John Hostetter.Tapes, John Deedy Gift, C-9 FilmB631991 - 1992B761992 - 1993B80Correspondence, Poetry, DiariesB92Photograph album including photographs of festivities and attendees of Daniel Berrigan 75th Birthday Celebration, 1996B99B. Oversize MaterialOversize Material2America is Hard to Find Poster1H. VideoInvestigation of a FlameDVD-120B61The Trial of the Catonsville NineV-209B61
The Trial of the Catonsville Nine [Preservation]
The Trial of the Catonsville Nine [Staff access]America is Hard to FindV-8151970B61I. AudioAmerica is Hard to Find (LP)SR-340681 phonographic records.1970Series III. Bound ManuscriptsPurgatorioBd. Ms. 1
Scope and Contents
Mass market paper back, Oxford University Press edition of the work of Dante Alighieri, title page and some other pages removed, personal copy of Philip Berrigan, with pencilled annotations. Book has been removed from binding and individual sections bound with tape.Demonstration photo albumBd. Ms. 2 +
Scope and Contents
Album of photographs of Philip Berrigan and other people at several sites; no dates given; photographer/s unknown; various signs and banners displayed; some photos depict interactions with police. There is also a work of graphic art by George Knowlton.Prison and the Christian Resister: The Letters and Journals from Prison of Daniel and Philip BerriganBd. Ms. 3 +
Scope and Contents
M.A. Thesis SUNY Buffalo, pertains to the lives and activities, prior to 1976, of Daniel and Philip Berrigan, Catholic priests active in peace and social justice causes by Sara J. McLaughlin.Religion and radicalism: the American "Catholic Left" as a social movementBd. Ms. 4 +
Scope and Contents
PhD dissertation prepared at Columbia University by Charles A. Meconis. Found (with papers from Daniel Berrigan of early 1981) in a box labelled (by Meconis?) "to Prof. Gilllian Lindt from: Charles A. Meconis PhD. (Cand.) re: Defense copy of dissertation." With the dissertation were found typed and handwritten comments by Elizabeth (McAlister) Berrigan, Philip Francis Berrigan, and Daniel Berrigan; these have been removed and separately cataloged under Berrigan, Elizabeth (McAlister). The title page was lacking, and Philip Berrigan's comments were written on the copyright page; therefore, the dissertation has been bound with replacement title page and copyright page. Cf. the book (N.Y.: Seabury 1979) by Charles A. Meconis, With clumsy grace: the American Catholic left, 1961-1975.[Unused numbers]Bd. Ms. 5 & 6The PetreanBd. Ms. 7 + v.1-3
Scope and Contents
Yearbook of St. Peter's Preparatory School, Jersey City, New Jersey, 1947-1949.Blue Book, 1957Bd. Ms. 8 +
Scope and Contents
Yearbook of Brooklyn Preparatory School, Brooklyn, N.Y.Pity the nation..Bd. Ms. 9 +
Scope and Contents
This is a bound script of a drama about a 1969 trial in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. The author has signed the manuscript with a note to Daniel Berrigan, by James Sage.Daniel Berrigan, S.J.: A bibliography of criticism, with an introductory essay on his critical reputationBd. Ms. 10 +
Scope and Contents
MA thesis prepared at the University of Maryland by Carol Ann Rittner. Thesis is concerned with interpretation and critical reception of Daniel Berrigan's published works.Daniel Berrigan scrapbookBd. Ms. 11 ++
Scope and Contents
Scrapbook contains newspaper clippings, photos, programs, and writings assembled by Daniel Berrigan in his youth. There is material about various Berrigan family members: John Berrigan, Jerome Berrigan, Edmund Berrigan, Agnes Berrigan, etc. There are early writings by Daniel Berrigan and a poem by John Berrigan. Selection of clippings prefigures some of Daniel Berrigan's adult interests in civil rights and resistance to civil authority when in conflict with religious idealism.Daniel Berrigan poetryBd. Ms. 12 +
Scope and Contents
Consists of a collection of Daniel Berrigan's poetic works, copied and bound together. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
